description:
Perform a Quality Assurance audit on the HEC-RAS Muncie model's Hydraulic Property Tables (HTabs). Your goal is to detect any cross-sections where conveyance does not increase monotonically with stage (a common source of model instability) and document the reach's hydraulic capacity.

Starting from the Muncie project directory with the HDF5 results file (`Muncie.p04.hdf`) available, write a Python script using `h5py` to:

1. Access the Hydraulic Property Tables in the HDF5 file (typically under `/Geometry/Cross Sections/Hydraulic Tables` or similar path).
2. For every cross-section, check if the Conveyance-Elevation curve is strictly monotonic (i.e., Conveyance[i+1] >= Conveyance[i]). Log any violations.
3. Identify the cross-section with the maximum flow capacity (highest maximum conveyance value).
4. Extract the full Stage vs. Conveyance curve for the most downstream cross-section (lowest River Station value).

Save your findings to a JSON file at `/home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/conveyance_audit.json` with the following structure:
